title: microseris paludosa
intro: Microseris paludosa, also known as marsh dandelion or western buttercup, is a perennial plant native to the Pacific coastal regions of North America, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Microseris paludosa include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support immune system function. Herbal preparations of Microseris paludosa can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used to aid in digestion and reduce symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
